tr.td: Alkaline phosphatase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma
Alanine aminotransferase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma
Aspartate aminotransferase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma
Cholesterol in LDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Direct assay
Cholesterol in HDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma

tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The results show elevated creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function, and low globulin, which may indicate decreased immune proteins or possible laboratory error (units seem inconsistent). Triglycerides are mildly elevated, indicating a risk for metabolic syndrome. Other parameters, including liver enzymes, glucose, and electrolytes, are within normal limits. Renal function and globulin levels should be further evaluated in the clinical context.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
